programme.desc: When Tia's out sick from school, Tamera befriends a new girl who becomes obsessed with looking and acting like the twins.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I Do?
programme.desc: Lisa gets "cold feet" after Terrence pops the question, while the twins worry that Lisa's marriage means they'll stop living under the same roof.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Natural
programme.desc: Tia rearranges her class schedule in order to spend more time with the school "stud"; Tamera is tapped to pitch a big softball game

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Break-Up
programme.desc: Lisa decides it's time to "dump" Terrence and forbids Ray from hanging out with him, while Roger tries to pick which twin to "woo."

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Tutor
programme.desc: When a high school football star needs a tutor, Tamera pretends to be Tia so she can teach him a special lesson in "chemistry"!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: History A La Cart
programme.desc: An embarrassing arrest from his college days comes back to haunt Ray, and Lisa turns an inheritance into a shopping spree

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Grandpa Campbell
programme.desc: When Ray's ne'er-do-well father comes for a visit, he turns the household upside-down -- and turns Tamera into a ruthless businesswoman!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Twins Get Fired
programme.desc: When Tia becomes Tamera's new boss at Rocket Burger, they both learn a lesson in responsibility

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Halloween
programme.desc: While the twins go in search of a Halloween rave, Lisa and Ray attend a fancy party thrown by a prospective client.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Weird Science
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera think they're in for a "free ride" when Ray starts dating their science teacher.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part I)
programme.desc: When Ray and Lisa take the girls to Hawaii for Thanksgiving, they're forced to share their condo with their "exes," who have begun dating each other.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Thanksgiving in Hawaii (Part II)
programme.desc: While Ray and Lisa frantically search for the twins, who are missing in Hawaii, Roger opens the Campbell/Landry home to hobos for Thanksgiving dinner.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Private School
programme.desc: The prospect of Tia's attending a prestigious prep school puts social pressure on Tia and financial pressure on Lisa.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Christmas
programme.desc: When a loan shark comes after Ray's father for a bad debt, the family comes to his rescue.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Double Date
programme.desc: The twins find themselves in double trouble when they switch identities and Tia winds up with two dates for the same party.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Reality Really Bites
programme.desc: For her film class, Tia "spices up" her family documentary by throwing all sorts of monkey wrenches into their lives.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Volunteers
programme.desc: While volunteering at a local retirement home, Tamera becomes determined to help a grumpy old man find happiness again.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Valentine's Day finds Tamera trying to "steal back" her hot date, and Ray and Lisa wondering if the other person is in love with them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Paper or Plastic?
programme.desc: Tamera crosses the picket lines to work at a supermarket, but soon realizes that being a "scab" isn't all it's cracked up to be.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Piano Lesson
programme.desc: Ray learns a "noteworthy" lesson in honesty when he "fibs" and tells a tone-deaf Tamera that she plays the piano well, and she winds up humiliating herself at a piano recital.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Summer Bummer
programme.desc: Tia and Tamera trade places so that Tia can be with a cute guy in summer school and Tamera can "goof off" by taking Tia's spot as a camp counselor.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Candidate
programme.desc: Tamera learns how much she needs Tia's help when she runs for class president.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Big Twin on Campus
programme.desc: When Tia poses as a college student to impress her older boyfriend, Tamera goes to great lengths to fit in with Tia's new circle of "mature" friends.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: The Audition
programme.desc: Tamera's budding career as a lowdown blues singer is threatened when she gets "too happy" after her dream date asks her out!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: When a Man Loves Two Women
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the twins, they've both got a crush on the same guy: Roger!
programme.sub-title: When a Man Loves Two Women

programme.title: Sister, Sister: You Are So Beautiful
programme.desc: Curiosity about their birth parents leads the twins, Ray, and Lisa on a quest to learn more about the girls' past.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Gimme a Brake
programme.desc: Tempted by the freedom of a new car, Tia and Tamera soon find themselves on a collision course with disaster when their car drops off a dock and into Lake Erie!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Daddys Girl
programme.desc: Tamera learns she's never too old to be a daddy's girl when she finds herself jealous of the growing closeness between Ray and Tia.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Sis Boom Bah
programme.desc: In a bid to boost their popularity at school, the twins try out for the cheerleading squad -- and Ray tries his hand at coaching them.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Kid-Napped
programme.desc: When a new girl in school threatens Tia and Tamera's popularity, the twins join forces to win back their friends with the help of a music superstar.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Boy From the Hood
programme.desc: Tamera falls for Tia's childhood friend Darnell, who's all grown up and running with a fast crowd.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: I'll be There
programme.desc: Tia takes a job in a '50s diner to get away from Tamera and is shocked when Tamera starts working there, too.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Ch-Ch-Ch-Changes
programme.desc: The twins are thrilled when Ray and Lisa decide to take their relationship to a more romantic level, but have second thoughts when Roger outlines the downsides of their parents dating.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Double Exposure
programme.desc: When Tia poses for Tamera's class picture, the gorgeous photo that results makes Tamera extremely popular, and Tia extremely resentful.

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Some Like it Hockey
programme.desc: Determined to make the school's all-male hockey team, Tia and Tamera must disguise themselves as boys and pretend they're just two of the guys!

programme.title: Sister, Sister: Bring on Debate
programme.desc: Ray feels left out by the close bond Tamera forms with her new music teacher.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU’s plan to take down a hostage holding gunman who is convinced that a restaurant owner is planning a terror attack.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU's attempt to take down a crime boss, but when the plan is botched, the boss flees, taking his girlfriend, a criminal Informant, with him.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: An outsider is brought in to perform psychological testing on Team One, but fearing someone will be let go, they start to turn on each other. Meanwhile, Ed is shot on the way to the hospital for the birth of his child.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One races to track down Ed's shooter, while his wife has problems delivering their baby.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One confronts a rioting crowd after a cop who mistakenly shot and killed a young boy is declared innocent.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One goes up against a notorious serial robber who streams his robberies on the internet.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: The SRU is called in when a mother and her daughter are abducted.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One struggles with a secret that could compromise their drug bust and end Wordy's career.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When Raf, a new recruit, joins Team One, he learns what it means to be a member of the Strategic Response Unit on one of their toughest days of the year forhostage and suicide situations.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Two bombs are set off while Sam, Spike and Raff are responding to a Hot Call at an office building, trapping them and nine civilians inside with a third seemingly non-diffusible bomb.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One has an unusual case when a passenger jet is hijacked by a group demanding the release of a prisoner.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One responds to 911 call where a masked gunman threatens a group of teens and kidnaps one of them.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One thinks they have a simple case of robbery on their hands,but things get complicated when the victim sabotages the rescue.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is alerted to a situation in a bank where a woman who appears under duress is withdrawing all her money. When she sabotages her own rescue and escapes with her tormentor, the team turn their attention to her family, who is also in danger.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: As Donna gets ready to walk down the aisle, things changed drastically. She learns some surprising news regarding a mentor she once worked with.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One must figure out how to get a Chinatown gang to release a local shop owner, then they also have to figure out how to get the one who shot the owner's daughter free as well.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: An ex-cop with a grudge against Greg Parker takes him hostage and threatens to drop him off an arena's megatron if Greg can't talk him down. Meanwhile his team discovers that they are helpless to use tactical options to save their boss.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: When Spike answers Sam's sister's desperate plea for help, he is forced, at gunpoint, to help two criminals steal evidence from the fortress that is the Metro Evidence Depot.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Team One is called in to try and stop a conflict at a psychiatric hospital where a stabbing has just taken place.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Greg Parker immediately faces suspension from the team after keeping the relationship between two team members a secret.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po fetches the other to watch Andy Brown and two children look for some rabbits. In Teletubbyland, Tinky Winky is looking for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies sit down for Tubby Toast. After they’ve eaten some more toast lands on their plates. They eat that, and some more toast lands again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a hiding game, Dipsy shuts his eyes and the other Tubbies hide from him. But Dipsy can't find them, so the Noo Noo helps him.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies play a hide & seek game, before watching a little boy with his stick insects. In Teletubbyland, Tinky Winky saw a big cloud, Po saw a small cloud and Dipsy and Laa-Laa see two medium size clouds.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind a hill to watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty sausage.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children take their dog for a walk. A voice trumpet appears and barks like a dog. Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po laugh as they run all over Teletubbyland barking.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy and Laa-Laa run into Home Hill to say 'Eh-oh', but where 's Po? Some children help their dad to wash his car. They get very wet and soapy.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and makes the sound of a whistle; Tinky Winky laughs when he hears the sound. The Tubbies watch some children who march and twirl their batons to music.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po jumps out from behind Tinky Winky and they run off to watch Andy Brown and some children looking for creatures. They find some woodlice under a log.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland, some voice trumpets rise and make some funny animal noises. They baa like sheep, moo like cows, meow like cats and oink like pigs.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A little girl goes to the shops to buy a Sari. The Noo Noo is tidying up the Teletubbies blankets, but Po doesn’t want the Noo Noo to tidy up her blanket.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa walks up to say “Eh-oh”. Then she goes to find Po, Dipsy and Tinky Winky and brings them in to say “Eh-oh” too. The windmill turns and the Tubbies watch a group of children go paddling in the sea.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little boy with his grandad’s pigeons. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies go out to play. They play on the hills spinning around and waving their arms.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children taking photographs of each other's faces. Dipsy has a good look around Teletubbyland when a telescope appears.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are feeling tired. Andy Brown and a little girl look for spiders in a garden. Two voice trumpets rise and tell Tinky Winky the nursery rhyme 'Incy Wincy Spider'.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children looking at penguins waddling and swimming. In Teletubbyland, the Tubbies are walking. They walk fast and slow, far and near and other different ways.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children making marks. They use paint and different objects to make a colourful picture.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po make some adjustments on the switch panel and laugh at the funny noises. The Tubbies watch Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty duck.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies see three children at a Mandir Temple where they are celebrating the Diwali festival. The Teletubbies have a very busy day. They watch the clouds, smell the flowers, look for rabbits and run round the house, but they are still not tired.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises to tell the Teletubbies ‘Christmas is a Coming’. They hurry up the slide to see some children decorate a Christmas Tree. The Teletubbies get excited when their own Christmas Tree appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the colour yellow. A magical new event happens in Teletubbyland – Little Bo Peep has lost her sheep, and doesn’t know where to find them.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl visit her cousin to stay the night. The Teletubbies are very busy with their favourite things.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children crawling. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are crawling. Tinky Winky crawled along the path, Dipsy crawled over the hill, Laa-Laa crawled between the trees and Po crawled around the flowers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up to say hello before they gather round to watch some children learn about the number nine. Po and Tinky Winky count nine flowers that appear in Teletubbyland and then run off to fin Dipsy and Laa-Laa and dance the Bump dance.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch two children looking through a telescope at the moon. In Teletubbyland, Po is going round and round.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies dance a walking dance. They stop to watch a group of children are riding on a rickshaw. In Teletubbyland, Po is riding her scooter.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies and a group of children see Tamzin Griffin pretend to be Funny Lady and tell the story of the naughty snake.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children exploring the colour red. Po makes some adjustments on the switch panel and all the lights change to the colour red. Po sees some red clouds and changes the colour of all the flowers to red.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Bryony loves to play the violin. She talks about her violin and they plays 'Twinkle Twinkle Little Star'. The Teletubbies sit down to listen to some lovely music, but the Noo Noo is making too much noise.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Teletubbies love to run and do exercises. The Tubbies watch a girl and a boy throwing a ball. Then they play a game together with the ball.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children dancing while King Pleasure and the Biscuit Boys play 'See Saw Majory Daw'. Po has fun going up and down the slide. The Lion and Bear appear in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies are very, very happy. They watch two boys make Chinese Lanterns with their uncle. In Teletubbyland, it is time for Tubby Toast, but where has Laa-Laa's toast gone?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and the Teletubbies run out of the house to listen to the Woof Woof sounds. The Teletubbies copy the sounds again and again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number two. Dipsy and Laa-Laa are eating Tubby Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Tubbies laugh when laugh when they bump into each other and fall over. They all watch two girls meet Tito a Flamenco guitar player who plays them a nursery rhyme. A guitar appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Dipsy hug and run off to watch some children making some fancy dress clothes. A crown appears in Teletubbyland and when Tinky Winky wears the crown he becomes King Tinky Winky.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children practise yoga. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies are very busy. Tinky Winky is marching with his bag, Dipsy is dancing with his hat, Laa-Laa is playing with her ball and Po is riding her scooter.

programme.title: Trains and Treehouses
programme.desc: Bob and his ‘Can-Do’ crew are back on site with a brand new adventure! Join Bob as he builds a train set for Mr Bentley as a birthday surprise and helps find a family of squirrels a new tree-house home.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are building Sunflower Valley’s first geodesic dome. As Wendy has organised the dome and knows how it all fits together, Bob suggests that she might like to take charge of the job. Reluctantly Wendy agrees.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles is having problems with mice eating his barley seed so he asks Bob to build a seed store to keep it safe. Whilst Bob is working, Farmer Pickles decides to go and buy some replacement seed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: A fierce storm is forecast for Bobsville. At the farm Travis stays safe and snug in his shed, while Spud takes cover in a large barn. The storm causes a lot of damage and a tree falls on Spud’s barn – he’s okay, but his nose is a bit squashed!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is rendering a wall for Mr Sabatini. The job is straightforward, but the finished result looks rather plain, so Bob offers to get some tiles from JJ’s yard to decorate it. While Bob is gone, Mr Sabatini demonstrates his soccer skills to Dizzy.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is erecting a tent to accommodate some newcomers to the valley. Mr Bentley is organising everything, but unfortunately leaves his phone with Spud who promptly declares himself ‘Mr Bentley’s Assistant’.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr. Beasley decides to grow pineapples, and the best way top do this is to build a pineapple pit – a special pit, heated by horse manure! Travis wants to help and decides to fetch some horse manure from Farmer Pickles farm in Bobsville as quick as he can.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are building the road connecting Bob’s mobile home site with the location of the new yard. It’s a big job for Roley, but just as Bob’s explaining the job, a pretty little bird flies overhead clutching some twigs in her beak.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Bentley is organising a winter fair in the town hall square and enlists Bob to help set up the stalls, lights and the centrepiece of the fair – a giant inflatable Santa Claus.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles has decided to open a camp site on his land, and Bob is installing a shower block and fence to keep the sheep out. For once, he is working on his own while Wendy goes on holiday with Jenny.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Train enthusiast Mr Bentley is away for the day and, in his absence, Mrs Bentley decides to surprise her husband by asking Bob to build him a brand new model railway in the shed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are all ready to set up Mr Sabatini’s bakery opposite the windmill so it’s close to the flour. Bob promises a surprise when they get there, and who should roll out from behind a bush but Benny!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Its early morning in Sunflower Valley and Benny arrives bursting with enthusiasm. He’s not needed in Bobsville today and he’s come to the Valley to help. Today, the team are building Mr and Mrs Bentley’s special new house.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The whole town is practicing for an egg and spoon race, which Mrs Percival has organised. Trix decides to enter, but unfortunately she practices with fresh eggs and in her usual haste she manages to make a great mess in JJ’s yard.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s Mr and Mrs Bentley’s twenty fifth wedding anniversary and to celebrate Mr Bentley asks Bob and the gang to build a special summer house. Mrs Bentley is out for the day and the summer house is to be a surprise.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang leave to convert a house into two separate flats. Mr Bentley, meanwhile, has been left in charge of The Mayor’s dog.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has been making secret early morning trips to the school where Mrs Percival has been giving him salsa lessons, as he wants to impress everybody at the town dance that night.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Pam Goody is running out of money to complete the Arts Centre renovations and Molly suggests holding a fashion show to raise funds. Pam thinks this a wonderful idea and asks Molly to organise it. Bob is, of course, just the man to build a catwalk.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and Dizzy have spent the night at Bob’s old yard in Bobsville to help Wendy with some digging and mixing. The machines all wish that Wendy could come and live out in Sunflower Valley.

programme.title: Built to be Wild
programme.desc: Yeeee-haaw! Its hoedowns and showdowns for Bob the Builder and his machine team as they hit the trail to the Wild West for their fourth feature length adventure - BUILT TO BE WILD!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr and Mrs Bentley are moving house, and Bob is carrying out a loft conversion at their new home. Mr Bentley greets Bob and his team, then leaves for work. The removal company call to say that they have several staff off with flu.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is replacing a potting shed on Mr Beasley’s allotment. In the old shed is a chest which Mr Beasley doesn’t need any more. Meanwhile Wendy calls at JJ’s for the materials for the new shed.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today’s the day that the machines get to build their brand new shelter. It’s going to be built into the side of a hill and made using found materials. Lofty, who slept outside at the old yard, decides that he’d like a place in the new shelter.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is demolishing an old house, and sends the floorboards and roof tiles for recycling. As he finishes the job, he gets a call from Mr Bentley who wants him to renovate the museum’s annexe, where Mr Ellis plans to exhibit the work of local artists.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in the local park. Farmer Pickles has picked up a selection of animals for it – including a chinchilla – and sends Travis and Spud off to deliver them to Bob. Unfortunately Bob runs out of chicken wire.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang have been asked to replace some burst pipes under the main road. Meanwhile Jenny is in town to persuade Wendy to take a much-needed holiday. Unfortunately, there’s lots of work to do and Wendy simply doesn’t have time to go away.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is repairing a leaking water tank at the school, when he discovers bats in the belfry. He tapes up the tank temporarily, but says the bats will need a new home as they are a protected species.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are finishing refurbishing the old watermill ready to begin milling the first Sunflower Valley flour. Work is just about to start when Dizzy hears a squeaking noise coming from above. There are bats in the old mill!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a marshland walkway with his Dad’s help. Benny hears that the marsh is ‘like a jungle’ and becomes determined to finds some jungle animals like tigers. Every time he spots one, however, it turns out to be something very ordinary.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a day off and he takes a trip to Farmer Pickles farm to take some photographs for the town photography competition. Wendy, meanwhile, has been called to the school hall where a pipe has burst, threatening the competition.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are drilling a special well at their homestead. The well has its own water tank, which needs to be raised from the ground on rocks. Wendy and Scrambler arrive for the first ever time in Sunflower Valley..

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Today Bob and the team have a very exciting job to do. They are building a bridge over the river to where Mr Sabatini’s new bakery will be. Bob promises Scrambler that he can be the first machine across the bridge.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has a plan to accommodate all of the newcomers. He’s designed a kit house that clips together quickly and easily. Bob’s dad Robert comes to the valley to help build the houses, while Mr and Mrs Bentley conduct a survey on the newcomers’ requirements.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop is keen to enter the town’s snowman competition, but a burst pipe outside Mr Sabatini’s needs fixing. Scoop is anxious when he sees the snowman that Mr Sabatini has made for the competition.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has been asked to install a new air conditioning unit at Mr Sabatini’s restaurant. Wendy and Molly meanwhile have been asked to redecorate the library at the school.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: An early morning start for the sunflower valley team, as they are moving logs in preparation for a safety fence around Mr Sabatini’s windmill. Wendy’s off to the sunflower oil factory to help Marjorie with a big delivery.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a new exercise park which Mr Dixon’s brother David, a famous goalkeeper, is going to open. Dizzy tries to cajole Spud into joining in a game of soccer, but uncharacteristically the scarecrow doesn’t want to play.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and crew are demolishing an old barn for Farmer Pickles, but as Lofty tests his demolition ball he accidentally damages one of the pillars at the entrance to Bob’s yard. Wendy offers to stay and repair this while Bob gets on with the day’s job.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a big day. The team are building Farmer Pickles new factory which will turn sunflowers into sunflower oil. Everyone is excited to meet Marjorie, who arrives by bicycle. Marjorie has been brought on board by Farmer Pickles to manage the factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Robert’s come to the valley to spend some quality time with Bob. They have a plan to spend the afternoon birdwatching – Robert wants to see the very rare lesser-striped sunflower warbler.

programme.title: Bob's Big Plan
programme.desc: When Bob and the team are building an extension to the offices of Mr Adams the local architect, Bob hears of a competition to build a brand new town in nearby Sunflower Valley, a place Bob knows very well from his childhood. Mr Adams shows Bob his idea.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a water feature in Mrs Potts’ garden, while Wendy and Scoop are demolishing a dry stone wall. Bob and the Machines are greatly amused by the statue Mrs Potts has chosen for her centrepiece.

programme.title: When Bob Became a Builder
programme.desc: It’s a beautiful day in Sunflower Valley and Bob and his machine team are building a new picnic area at the beach. Dizzy, Lofty and Scrambler are clearing stuff that’s been washed up on the beach, while Benny has a great time digging with Scoop and Muck.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob, Scoop and Skip are helping Mr Beasley to install a new Rockery when Mr Bentley appears to ask for Bob’s help. The Mayor wants to stage an art exhibition, but there are no available venues. Scoop suggests renovating the inside of the Old Mill.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The team are building a bunkhouse for Farmer Pickles guests The Pickers! Micky, Vicky and Little Ricky are helping with the sunflower harvest. The cabin is to be made of logs so Lofty’s job is very important today, lifting and laying the logs.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s built a new waterwheel for the old watermill. He’s made it at the yard, but now needs to dismantle it to take it to the mill in sections. Bob needs Benny, Scoop, Lofty and Muck to carry the wheel sections through the marshland to the mill.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a blistering hot day on Sunflower Valley. Bob and Wendy have got to finish building their workshop – making the workbenches and installing the power tools – it’s an inside job so the machines all get the day off.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the gang are building a pedestrian crossing. Mrs Percival, meanwhile, has asked Molly to paint a mural of a jungle scene in the school playground.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles has a new family of Pigs being delivered to his farm and Bob must build a new home for them. In the meantime, Farmer Pickles places the pigs in an old Barn to keep them safe.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s job today is to repair an eroded riverbank. There will be LOTS of mud to shift and Muck is beside himself with excitement. Before Muck can get stuck in, Bob needs him to fetch some equipment from JJ’s.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a goat run for Farmer Pickles new goat – Giddy. It’s Travis’s job to collect Giddy from Meg MacDonald the goat breeder. Spud teases Travis about being slow and Travis resolves to collect the goat very quickly.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Ellis has a batch of Doves delivered, to use in his magic act, and asks Bob to build a dovecote for his new birds to live in. Mr Ellis realises that he has to go to work but does not want to leave the birds at home alone.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Scoop and the machines are in Sunflower Valley setting up Wendy’s caravan. Scoop contacts Muck to find out where Bob has got to. Muck explains that they haven’t left Bobsville yet because they’ve lost Bob’s Dad!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are installing a bull-rush water recycling system to clean water from Farmer Pickles’ cottage. The clean water will be used to water Farmer Pickles new orchard. Bob explains that everything needs to be built in order – it’s a process.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s too busy to even tell the machines what the job is for today. He takes Scrambler to the dome and the others follow. It turns out that they are going to turn the whole of the Dome into a Visitors centre and its opening tomorrow!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Wendy and Bob have a very special announcement to make. They are going to fix up an old Houseboat for Wendy to live in. There’s a lot of hard work to be done. They need to make a gang plank, fix solar panels and give it a really good clean.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Mr Beasley’s pipes and boiler are making banging noises and Bob is called in to replace them. Whilst Bob and Wendy are collecting the new Boiler from JJ, Molly explains that she is looking after Mrs Broadbent’s baby rabbits.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: There is great excitement at Bob’s yard when his new metal detector is delivered. He plans to use it to find a burst pipe in Farmer Pickles’ field, and promises Muck that afterwards they will hunt for buried treasure.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a Seaweed Farm for Annie Pickles – Farmer Pickles cousin. Scrambler volunteers to help Annie collect seaweed seedling on the beach.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Team are converting Mr Sabatini’s windmill into a café. Roley is rolling the ground flat for all the tables and chairs while Bob makes the awnings. Roley hits trouble when molehills keep appearing where he’s just been rolling.

programme.title: Green Builds and Recycling Centres
programme.desc: Bob’s back On Site! Join Bob as he shows you the amazing world of eco homes and recycling centres and learn how things you throw away can be used to make something new.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Now that Robert has taken over at the old yard, Wendy can finally move to Sunflower Valley! Bob is still in Bobsville, while the machines are in charge of setting up Wendy’s caravan, complete with solar panels, as a special surprise.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Whilst Bob and the team build Annie Pickles new seafront house, Muck is worried that Scoop is a better digger than he is and decides to prove himself with a sandcastle competition.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: It’s a big day, as the team are building Bob’s new workshop at his new yard. Scoop is desperate to get stuck in and beavers about doing everything he can to help. Bob needs some old floorboards collecting from Bobsville, and sends Scoop to pick them up.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Murder Of Stephen Cameron
programme.desc: Gripping documentary examining the fatal stabbing of 21-year-old motorist Stephen Cameron during a motorway altercation in 1996.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Dunblane Massacre
programme.desc: In an emotional tribute, witnesses and victims' relatives look back on the tragic murder of 16 children and a teacher at Dunblane Primary School in Scotland, 15 years ago.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Rochdale Groomers
programme.desc: Rochdale Groomers: Documentary examining the sex abuse scandal which occurred in the North of England and appeared to go undetected for quite some time.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephanie Slater
programme.desc: Estate agent Stephanie Slater reveals the story of her abduction, rape and incarceration, which occurred in 1992 whilst showing a man around a house.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Lawrence
programme.desc: On the 20th anniversary of his death, the family of Stephen Lawrence reflect on his tragic murder in 1993, as well as their lengthy fight for justice.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Raoul Moat
programme.desc: The hunt for Raoul Moat was one of the largest in British history, after he wounded his ex-girlfriend, killed her new partner and shot and blinded police constable David Rathband.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: April Jones
programme.desc: The hunt for five year old April Jones was the largest missing persons search in UK police history when she vanished in October 2012. But on October 6th a local man, Mark Bridger, was charged with her disappearance.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Sarah Payne
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Sarah Payne's disappearance and murder shocked the nation. Here, Sarah's mum reveals her tireless campaign to protect other children from paedophilia.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Colin Ireland
programme.desc: In the summer of 1993, gay men in London were being terrorised by a brutal killer. Colin Ireland, a loner from Southend, had made a macabre resolution – to become a serial killer.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Jimmy Savile
programme.desc: Jimmy Savile: From national treasure to one of the UK's most notorious sex offenders: this is the story of how the revelation of Jimmy Savile's crimes left the UK reeling.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Philpotts
programme.desc: When six children died in a house fire in Allenton, Derby in May 2012 it shocked the nation. Parents Mick and Mairead Philpott were charged with the murders. We uncover the reasons behind the arson attack on their own home.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Joanne Dennehy
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan returns to uncover the truth behind the horrific crimes of Peterborough serial killer Joanna Dennehy.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Wrongly Released
programme.desc: This special episode shines a light on criminals who have been brought to justice for one crime only to be released to commit further terrible acts.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Marchioness Disaster
programme.desc: The story of the Marchioness; a pleasure boat that collided with dredger Bowbelle and sank within minutes resulting in the loss of 51 lives.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Stephen Port
programme.desc: A chilling look at the crimes of Stephen Port, the east London serial killer who poisoned four young men with lethal doses of a date rape drug.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: John Sweeney
programme.desc: John Sweeney brutally murdered two of his girlfriends, dismembered their bodies and dumped them in canals. In a macabre twist, he hid clues in his abstract paintings, and five other girlfriends of his are currently still missing.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Russell Murders
programme.desc: Bludgeoned to death whilst walking down a country lane, the horrific murders of Lin and Megan Russell made worldwide news. Discover the events of that shocking day.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: Christopher Foster
programme.desc: Failed businessman Christopher Foster killed his wife and daughter before burning their mansion in Shropshire to the ground in August 2008.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Stiff Good Man is Easy to Find
programme.desc: William’s girlfriend encourages him to cheat on her, and Toni can’t decide whether to marry Todd or dump him.
programme.sub-title: A Stiff Good Man is Easy to Find

programme.title: Girlfriends: Runaway Bridesmaid
programme.desc: Ellis suspects Joan’s unhappiness has more to do with Toni’s wedding than their relationship.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Single Mama Drama
programme.desc: Maya makes a new friend that her old friends don’t like, and Lynn fakes her birthday to collect on a cash prize.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Pact
programme.desc: Joan’s former romantic rival drops a bombshell, and William’s former boss dangles a tantalizing offer in his face.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Where Everyone Knows My Name
programme.desc: Maya is upset after she sees Darnell with a new woman.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Too Much Sharkin
programme.desc: Joan blows the lid off William’s office romance.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Wedding, Part I

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Wedding, Part II

programme.title: Girlfriends: Some Enchanted Evening

programme.desc: Toni’s husband is hiding a costly secret.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Snoop, There It Is
programme.desc: Lynn snoops into Sivad’s past.

programme.title: Girlfriends: You Ain't Gotta Go Home, But…You know The Rest!

programme.sub-title: You Ain't Gotta Go Home, But…You know The Rest!

programme.desc: Ellis’ return forces Joan to confront her feelings for Brock.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Inherit the Lynn
programme.desc: Lynn moves in with Maya.

programme.title: Girlfriends: And Baby Makes Four
programme.desc: William has second thoughts about fatherhood.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Viva Las Vegas
programme.desc: Joan hears wedding bells – but are they all in her mind?

programme.title: Girlfriends: Between Brock and a Hard Place
programme.desc: Brock shocks Joan with news that could end their engagement.
programme.sub-title: Between Brock and a Hard Place

programme.title: Girlfriends: Don't You Want Me Baby?
programme.desc: When Toni revisits the past, it could destroy her future with Todd.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Merry Ex-Mas
programme.desc: Maya claims she’s over Darnell – but is she?

programme.title: Girlfriends: Prophet and Loss
programme.desc: Joan and William are haunted by the words of a stranger who’s either a psychic – or a psycho.

programme.desc: Valentine’s Day finds Toni meddling in Joan’s love life, and William hoping to make a date with destiny

programme.title: Girlfriends: Leggo My Ego
programme.desc: Toni’s obsessed with meeting supermodel Beverly Johnson.

programme.desc: Maya fears her major-league beau is a real player.
programme.sub-title: Good Catch or a Bad Hop?

programme.title: Girlfriends: On the Couch
programme.desc: Toni must choose between her husband and her mother.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Love, Peace & Hair Grease
programme.desc: Joan’s in for a big surprise when she contemplates quitting her job

programme.title: Girlfriends: Wieners and Losers
programme.desc: After quitting the law firm, Joan goes from the courtrooms to food courts.

programme.title: Girlfriends: He Loves Her, He Loves Me Not
programme.desc: William’s new girlfriend claims he is in love with Joan.
programme.sub-title: He Loves Her, He Loves Me Not

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Partnerless Partner
programme.desc: William’s career may be in high gear, but his love life is stuck in park.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Just the Three of Us
programme.desc: Toni’s breakthrough in therapy could push her marriage to the breaking point

programme.title: Girlfriends: Love Thy Neighbor
programme.desc: While Lynn and Maya face off over a man, Toni fights to save her marriage.

programme.title: Girlfriends: New York Bound (2 part)
programme.desc: Joan comes to terms with her feelings for William, and Toni stuns Todd with an unexpected announcement.

programme.title: Girlfriends: New York Unbound
programme.desc: Joan comes to terms with her feelings for William, and Toni stuns Todd with an unexpected announcement.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The Rabbit Died
programme.desc: Toni’s friends think she’s lying about her pregnancy, and Maya causes trouble for Reverend Al Sharpton.

programme.title: Girlfriends: L.A. Bound
programme.desc: The Girlfriends are heading back to L.A. from New York – and they’re in for a bumpy ride.

programme.title: Girlfriends: A Mile in Her Loubous
programme.desc: Maya’s cousin wants a piece of her book deal.

programme.title: Girlfriends: The J Spot
programme.desc: Joan decides to open a restaurant.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Maybe Baby
programme.desc: Toni’s husband asks for a divorce.

programme.title: 6 Below
programme.desc: An adrenaline seeking snowboarder gets lost in a massive winter storm in the back country of the High Sierras. Starring: Josh Hartnett, Mira Sorvino, Sarah Dumont. It is based on the book Crystal Clear by Eric LeMarque and Davin Seay.

programme.title: 16 Blocks
programme.desc: From director of action blockbuster Lethal Weapon, Richard Donner. Personal demons put cop Jack (Bruce Willis - Die Hard) at rock bottom but he wasn’t about to let a witness be murdered on his watch.

programme.title: Then Came You
programme.desc: A lonely widow plans a trip around the world with her husband's ashes, to visit the places they loved in the movies. The first stop on the journey changes her life forever.

programme.title: The Cannonball Run
programme.desc: The classic comedy with cool stars and even cooler cars! Based on a true race event, Burt Reynolds leads an all-star cast in the ultimate quest to win The Cannonball Run.

programme.title: God's Not Dead
programme.desc: College philosophy professor Mr. Radisson's curriculum is challenged by his new student, Josh, who believes God exists. Starring:Shane Harper, Kevin Sorbo, David A.R. White

programme.title: Legendary
programme.desc: Cal Chetley is a bright, engaging, undersized fifteen year-old who's been picked on most of his life. Cal joins his high school wrestling team in the hopes his brother will train him. More importantly, Cal uses wrestling as the tool to reunite his family.

programme.title: The Long Walk Home
programme.desc: One black and one white woman in 1955 Montgomery, Alabama must decide what they are going to do in response to the famous bus boycott led by Martin Luther King. With Whoopi Goldberg and Sissy Spacek.

programme.title: A Good Woman
programme.desc: The seductive Mrs. Erlynne (Helen Hunt) travels to Amalfi, Italy, hoping to win over married aristocrat Robert Windermere. But when Windermere’s innocent wife Meg (Scarlett Johansson) hears of the scandal, she plots an elaborate revenge.

programme.title: Mr Morgan's Last Love
programme.desc: He's a widower in Paris who speaks no French. She's a dance instructor less than half his age. Can they become a family, or will his estranged adult children halt the friendship?

programme.title: 25 Years of Tiger
programme.desc: A look back at Tiger Woods' legendary pro career.

programme.title: The CUT
programme.desc: A preview of the John Deere Classic and look back at Aldrich Potgeiter's win in Detroit at the Rocket Classic.

programme.title: PGA TOUR Backspin
programme.desc: Watch the latest highlights from the best players in the world from courses around the PGA TOUR.

programme.title: PGA Tour Originals
programme.desc: An exclusive look at one of the most iconic seasons in golf. Tiger Woods changed golf history in 2000, securing nine wins, including three major championships, and breaking several records. 25 years later, TIGER 2000 revisits that legendary season.

programme.title: PGA TOUR - The Drop
programme.desc: A pair of phenoms in Luke Clanton and Michael Thorbjornsen are spotlighted ahead of the John Deere Classic.

programme.title: The CUT
programme.desc: A preview of the John Deere Classic and look back at Aldrich Potgeiter's win in Detroit at the Rocket Classic.

programme.title: Aldrich Potgieter Wins the 2025 Rocket Classic
programme.desc: Aldrich Potgieter captures the Rocket Classic and becomes the youngest South African to win on TOUR.

programme.title: 2013 John Deere Classic - Final Round
programme.desc: The final round of the 2013 John Deere Classic, where 19-year-old Jordan Spieth defeated Zach Johnson and David Hearn in a five-hole playoff to become the youngest PGA TOUR winner since 1931.

programme.title: 2023 John Deere Classic - Final Round
programme.desc: The final round of the 2023 John Deere Classic, where Austrian Sepp Straka won his 2nd PGA TOUR event.

programme.title: The Starter
programme.desc: Catch up on all the latest happenings on the PGA TOUR with feature content on the best players in the world.

programme.title: 2013 John Deere Classic - Final Round
programme.desc: The final round of the 2013 John Deere Classic, where 19-year-old Jordan Spieth defeated Zach Johnson and David Hearn in a five-hole playoff to become the youngest PGA TOUR winner since 1931.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: Shawn is called to the Natural History Museum when a mummy goes missing and all indications are that it got up and walked out on its own.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: Shawn's mother Madeleine is back to conduct psych evaluations for the police, and her return to old habits has Shawn unsettled. Meanwhile, Gus can't help out as he's dealing with his own troubles at work.

programme.title: Psych
programme.desc: Gus has worked long and hard organizing their high school reunion, so when Shawn claims one of their fellow alumni has been murdered and his body has been hidden, Gus refuses to believe him.

programme.title: Scorpion: Hard Knox
programme.desc: Team Scorpion is hired by the Department of Defense to break into Fort Knox and "steal" a precious artifact in order to test their security.

programme.title: Scorpion: Chernobyl Intentions
programme.desc: Team Scorpion goes to Chernobyl to repair the decaying nuclear reactor before a catastrophic meltdown.

programme.title: Scorpion: Toby Or Not Toby
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must out-think their unstable ex-Scorpion member after he kidnaps Toby and threatens to kill him unless his cunning demands are met.

programme.title: Skins: Katie and Emily
programme.desc: Emily disguises herself as her sister when she refuses to leave the house.

programme.title: Skins: Finale
programme.desc: Cook and Effy stay with Cook's father as they continue their self-imposed exile from the gang, while JJ tries to snap Freddie out of his depressed stupor.

programme.title: Skins: Thomas
programme.desc: Thomas turns to God when he feels responsible for an accident at the club.

programme.title: Skins: Emily
programme.desc: Emily and Naomi are interviewed by the police after they are listed as close friends of Sophia.

programme.title: Skins: Cook
programme.desc: Cook pleads not guilty and is out on bail after beating someone up at a party.

programme.title: Skins: Katie
programme.desc: Katie turns to Emily to try to help their mother.

programme.title: Mr. D: Coach of the Year
programme.desc: Gerry figures he’s a sure thing for the school’s Coach of the Year award after his team wins the city championship.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry’s Redemption
programme.desc: After losing his Xavier Academy job last season over the unfortunate Coach Keenan saga, Gerry finds himself subbing at a tough inner-city school.

programme.title: Mr. D: Educational Assistant
programme.desc: Gerry is assigned an educational assistant, Marty, who quickly becomes a new source of irritation in the classroom.

programme.title: Mr. D: Wrestling For Love
programme.desc: When Lisa asks Gerry to cover coaching duties for the upcoming wrestling tournament, Gerry promises to bring home two trophies.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Runs A Sweatshop
programme.desc: Gerry hatches a new "get rich quick" scheme selling counterfeit One Direction merchandise when he learns the band is in town.

programme.title: Mr. D: Re-Tired of Marking
programme.desc: When Gerry is suddenly promoted from sub to full-time teacher, his initial thrill is quickly squashed by the realization that he's back to real teaching responsibilities.

programme.title: Mr. D: Duncan vs. Bailey
programme.desc: When Gerry learns that former Olympic champion Donovan Bailey is coming to town, he's determined to seek retribution for a long-standing bone he has with the track "star".

programme.title: Mr. D: Student Teacher
programme.desc: When Gerry and Simon get student teachers, Gerry's hands-off approach fails; Bobbi starts taking supplements; Emma's characters bear a resemblance to some of the staff.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Wants A Union
programme.desc: Gerry decides to form a teachers union at Xavier; Malik bails on his janitorial duties; Lisa has a crush on Trudy's brother.

programme.title: Mr. D: Duncan Does Detention
programme.desc: Gerry is forced into covering Saturday detention; Robert and Lisa have a heart to heart.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Coaches Fencing
programme.desc: Gerry is assigned to help Ricki prepare for a fencing tournament; Bobbi invites Dwyer over for dinner in an attempt to seduce him.

programme.title: Mr. D: Hell-icopter Mom
programme.desc: Gerry is confronted by his overprotective and obnoxious mother; Bobbi tries to pawn off the role of staff punching bag; Ricki sells pencils to Robert.

programme.title: Mr. D: Out Cold
programme.desc: Gerry meets a beautiful woman while on a leadership retreat; Lisa learns of Trudy's gambling ring; Simon and Frank's game takes an ugly turn.

programme.title: Mr. D: Gerry Turns 40
programme.desc: Upon his 40th birthday, Gerry finds a letter he wrote to himself 10 years ago; Simon's ferret goes missing; Lisa tries to impress the chairman of the board of trustees.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: A new inter-dimensional portal system for human travel turns out to be something more when the resistance discover a CVI in a boy who got lost in the system.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Infection
programme.desc: Ne'eg is killed by a bacteria that is both deadly to Taelons and Humans. If it spreads, it might be disastrous. A new racist group attacks a community center.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Destruction
programme.desc: The Liberation sends a team to destroy the scarecrow probe before the Taelons crack it. Joshua Doors is appointed as secretary of Human-Taelon relations.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Joining
programme.desc: An excavation team unleashes a very dangerous Taelon on Earth. Siobhan Beckett comes over to America. She suspects Lili of being a spy and investigates.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: First of its Kind
programme.desc: Following Boone's confrontation with Hegel that left him severely injured, he was eliminated by Zo'or. Beckett who was impregnated by Hegel gives birth to a boy who immediately grows up to a man.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Avatus
programme.desc: Da'an looses his contact with the the Taelon Commonality. This causes him to change and Zo'or instructs that Da'an must be killed; because he's now considered a threat.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: A Stitch in Time
programme.desc: An exhibition for the first arrival of the Taelons showcases different artifacts. An artifact causes Liam to travel to an alternate time. Zo'or questions how Da'an was able to rejoin the Commonality.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Dimensions
programme.desc: Liam and Augur are in a Taelon shuttle craft, when something happens they get thrown into a parallel universe. They discover that humanity is at war with the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Moonscape
programme.desc: Augur is forced to take part in a Taelon experiment. Da'an and Liam try to convince Zo'or that altering minds and implanting humans to make them aggressive to fight the Jaridians is not the correct path. Maiya makes contact with Sandoval.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: The Sleepers
programme.desc: A Jaridian probe lands on Earth and begins sucking energy from the Comonality. The Taelons use humans in a near-death state to keep their connection while Lili and Sandoval try to find out what is draining the energy.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Fissures
programme.desc: When large scale blackouts begin to occur in cities with portals, Taelon technology is blamed. Augur and Liam investigate the death of a portal worker by a strange creature that feeds on energy and is particularly attracted to the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Redemption
programme.desc: Siobhan falls while rock climbing. As the others search for her, she dreams that she is on trial for supporting the Taelons. Zo'or questions whether Sandoval's CVI imperative to serve the Taelons has broken down as others are experiencing.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Between Heaven and Hell
programme.desc: Dr. Belman's daughter does human testing on herself with an accelerant that causes her to rapidly evolve. Joshua Doors decides somebody should run against President Thompson in the election, somebody who will stand up to the Taelons.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Isabel
programme.desc: Maiya experiences dimensional phasing. Augur and Liam believe they need to bring Isabel and Maiya together to let the universe merge them. Jonathan Doors announces his presidential candidacy and meets with Zo'or.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict
programme.desc: An escaped Jiridian takes Lili hostage. Their shuttle is sucked on to a Kimera ship where they, with Liam, have to go through an obstacle course to get to the core to exit.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict
programme.desc: Using Jiridian technology, scientists create a physical bio surrogates where a human consciousness can be transferred. When a surrogate escapes, they must find it before it uses its fighting and weapons knowledge.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Second Chances
programme.desc: A Taelon program offers a transformation into a younger, healthier version of oneself. Liam fears that something more dangerous is also happening. Lili is worried that Doors' run for the presidency is negatively affecting the resistance.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Payback
programme.desc: A mysterious death by poisoning creates questions about the safety of portal technology. When a Taelon is killed in the same manner, the investigation leads to a very smart scientist that even the Resistance was scared to work with.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Friendly Fire
programme.desc: Paul Chandler plots an attack on the Taelon Embassy in DC with the ship he stole from Lili. He begs her to join him in beginning an all out war.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Volunteers
programme.desc: A group of Resistance capture a volunteer from a Taelon program for youth. Liam decides they may learn valuable information from her. They also decide to send in one of their own as a volunteer to see what the program is teaching.

programme.title: Earth: Final Conflict: Bliss
programme.desc: Lili is given a psychotropic drug called "bliss" via a Taelon device. They discover it is widespread. With an instantly strong addictive property, the addicts are dying within 48 hours. The investigation leads Liam and Sandoval to Ireland.

programme.title: The Lost World: Trophies
programme.desc: A hunting party arrives on the plateau and takes fire at a Troglodyte child. Though they claim to be there to bring them home, Veronica becomes the target of their gunfire and snares.

programme.title: The Lost World: Voodoo Queen
programme.desc: An alluring stranger named Danielle seeks aide from Roxton and the others after her village is taken by Trogs. But when the woman uses a voodoo doll to bewitch Roxton, the group must rescue her.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Guardian
programme.desc: Challenger is trapped and taken to a village of orphans, protected by dense jungle and a plant that feeds on fresh meat. Marguerite and Veronica must help Challenger escape before they are killed.

programme.title: The Lost World: Under Pressure
programme.desc: A handsome stranger stumbles onto the plateau and becomes close with the explorers. While he gains the attention of Marguerite and Veronica, Challenger's electrostatic accumulator is targeted.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Outlaw
programme.desc: Roxton and Marguerite discover a way through the mountain and explore the fissure in hopes of finding a way off the plateau. Instead, they discover an English town and trouble with the locals.

programme.title: The Lost World: Quality of Mercy
programme.desc: Veronica and Malone are imprisoned at a labor camp occupied by emotionless prisoners, robotic wardens, and alien rulers called Prin. The explorers must plan a prison break to free their cohorts.

programme.title: The Lost World: Mark of the Beast
programme.desc: Marguerite and Roxton discover a strange amulet that leaves the mark of a dinosaur on their skin. Those with the imprint begin to tap into their more primitive instincts.

programme.title: The Lost World: Survivors
programme.desc: The explorers discover an ancient Temple, where they face off against mystical and experience hallucinations. One of these visions gives them a map that leads Challenger to William Maple White.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Pirate's Curse
programme.desc: A seismic event opens the earth and send Marguerite and Roxton into a pit, where they discover a pirate's buried treasure...and his bones. A curse on the booty complicates matters.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Visitor
programme.desc: After Malone is attacked by a beast, he and Veronica meet her old friend Danu, who has also been injured. Danu goes missing, and the group travels to his village learning he is a werewolf.

programme.title: The Lost World: A Man of Vision
programme.desc: The five adventurers all experience visions of Summerlee, the companion they had presumed dead after he disappeared into a waterfall. They suffer injury and face danger as they pursue the vision.

programme.title: The Lost World: Into the Fire
programme.desc: The incredible occurs when a Navy airship flies over the plateau. Malone manages to climb aboard, but is ambushed once inside and meets the deceptive Captain Askwith.

programme.title: The Lost World: Out of the Blue
programme.desc: Marguerite, Roxton, and Challenger take the place of Askwith aboard his ghost ship and return to the day of its fatal departure in 1915. Back on the Plateau, Veronica chases down Askwith.

programme.title: The Lost World: The Travelers
programme.desc: Malone remains missing, and the explorers' search party crosses paths with a trio of travelers. Marguerite becomes taken with the dashing leader, especially when he offers her a way off the Plateau.

programme.title: The Lost World: Eye for an Eye
programme.desc: Roxton learns what it's like to be the hunted after he kills a Triceratops. Osina-a mystical, half-dinosaur guardian-puts a curse on him that makes him the target of a mysterious hunter.

programme.title: The Lost World: True Spirit

programme.title: The Lost World: The Knife

programme.title: The Lost World: Fire in the Sky

programme.title: The Lost World: Dead Man's Hill

programme.title: Catfish UK: Leanne & Jamie
programme.desc: Leanne is desperate to find out if online love Jamie is her Mr Right. After cancelling on her numerous times, Leanne wants Oobah and Nella to investigate and finally bring them face to face. (S2, ep3)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Cole, Abbie & The Fanpage
programme.desc: Cole and Abbie keep being sent anonymous creepy gifts to their home and their relationship is now at breaking point. Julie and Oobah head to Scotland to try and help save their marriage. (S1, ep4)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Emma & Harlin
programme.desc: Julie and Oobah uncover more than they were expecting, as their search to help Emma find her online love Harlin takes a sinister turn. (S1, ep1)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Jerry & Karla
programme.desc: Jerry needs help meeting his first love Karla. Karla claims to have already met Jerry but refuses to talk to him on the phone. Can Oobah and Nella find out if Karla is who she says she is? (S2, ep1)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Lads Trip
programme.desc: Megan welcomes a new addition to the family... a horse! Chloe heads to the gym while Jordan heads to Amsterdam. There are fireworks for Amber and Ste, and Sassi plans a Christening. (S2, ep6)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Making Memories
programme.desc: Sassi almost misses her own Christening, Amber helps Ste celebrate his big day, Chloe remembers what it's like being a teenager, Megan makes a decision and Mia is left feeling frustrated. (S2, ep7)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Now Or Never
programme.desc: In the season finale it's make or break for Megan and Dylan and you'll never guess who's going on a date! As Sassi boards a plane, Mia jumps out of one. Chloe treats herself, big time. (S2, ep8)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Make Or Break
programme.desc: Welcome to the world Teen Mom UK series 3. Join our favourite five feisty Brit teen mums for access all areas into their worlds of toddlers, tiffs, tapas and tough love! (S3, ep1)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: The Ex Factor
programme.desc: Fireworks tonight as Amber decides she loves Ste, Erin stays the night at Manley's, Sassi hates single life, Megan tries to fix her family feud and Chloe and Jordan argue about nursery. (S3, ep2)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Enough Is Enough
programme.desc: It's all kicking off as Chloe loses it with Jordan, Sassi breaks down over Darren, Amber's confession backfires, Megan's in a bad place and Mia has something to say to Manley's mum. (S3, ep3)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Emotions And Explosions
programme.desc: Expect fireworks as Sassi sparkles on the catwalk, Amber tries to dazzle Ste, Megan's lost her spark, Mia launches her single life and Chloe gives Jordan a rocket! (S3, ep4)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: New Direction
programme.desc: It's tears, fears and changing gears! Sassi drives her mum to distraction, Chloe's dream home gives her nightmares, Megan and Mia go it alone and Amber and Ste get cosy. (S3, ep5)

programme.title: Teen Mom UK: Happy Ever After
programme.desc: Big changes all round. A new home for Chloe and Jordan, new love interests for Mia and Sassi, a change of plans for Megan, and Amber awaits some life changing news. (S3, ep6)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Alex & Matt
programme.desc: Alex has been chatting to Matt for a year and thinks he could be the one. But after several failed attempts to meet, Alex wants Julie and Oobah to track him down. (S1, ep5)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Tasheeka & Aaron
programme.desc: After avoiding video calls for 2 years, Tasheeka is desperate to find out if Aaron is her Prince Charming. It's up to Julie and Oobah to see if this online romance has a fairy tale ending. (S1, ep3)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Alayah & Tommy
programme.desc: Alayah and Tommy started messaging on a live streaming platform. Alayah thinks he could be the one, but when they try and meet, he keeps cancelling. Nella and Oobah help to track him down. (S2, ep8)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Taylor & Brad
programme.desc: Taylor is falling in love with Brad despite never having seen his face. Can Oobah and Nella find the man of her dreams, and will Taylor get her happy ever after? (S2, ep6)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Hayley & Alex
programme.desc: Oobah is joined by Catfish US royalty Kamie to help Hayley finally meet Alex. But after five months of avoiding phone calls and cancelling on her, will she get her a fairy tale ending? (S2, ep4)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Mario & Hannah
programme.desc: After turning his life around, Mario met his soulmate, Hannah. But she refuses to speak on the phone and won't meet in person. Julie and Oobah try to find out who his dream girl really is. (S1, ep7)

programme.title: Catfish UK: Emma & Harry
programme.desc: After six months, single mum Emma and online love Harry have already set a wedding date. But the problem is, they've never met. So it's up to the Catfish UK team to track him down. (S1, ep8)

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Law and Mrs. Shelton
programme.desc: A report on Catherine Mehaffey Shelton, a criminal defense attorney from Dallas. She has been practicing law in Texas for nearly three decades and making headlines for nearly as long, primarily because strange things happen to the men in her life.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Death at Cottonwood Creek
programme.desc: Frederick Mueller says while he and his wife were out for a walk near their vacation home, she fell off a cliff while he stopped to take a photo of her. Was it murder or an accident?

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: A New York dentist denies killing his lover's husband -- who was also his best friend.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Dog Trainer, The Heiress, and the Bodyguard - Part 1
programme.desc: When a celebrity dog trainer is murdered, an heiress fears for her life and a bodyguard vows to be a hero.
programme.sub-title: The Dog Trainer, The Heiress, and the Bodyguard - Part 1

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Dog Trainer, The Heiress, and the Bodyguard - Part 2
programme.desc: When a celebrity dog trainer is murdered, an heiress fears for her life and a bodyguard vows to be a hero.
programme.sub-title: The Dog Trainer, The Heiress, and the Bodyguard - Part 2

programme.title: 48 Hours: The One Who Got Away
programme.desc: A report about how serial killer Andrew Urdiales was identified, arrested and convicted, and about the young woman who became the only one of his victims to survive.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Murder at Sea?
programme.desc: The disappearance of newlywed George Smith, who vanished from a cruise in the Mediterranean. This report uncovers the questionable alibis, failed polygraphs and a provocative video that involves the three men who last saw Smith alive.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: When a 17-year-old girl breaks up with her abusive boyfriend, he snaps and breaks into her bedroom to attack her.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Deadly Ride
programme.desc: Jane Mixer, a student at the University of Michigan Law School in March of 1969, had just agreed to get married and had arranged a ride home to tell her parents. Her body was found in a small, rural cemetery just miles from her dorm.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Preppy Killer
programme.desc: Robert Chambers speaks out after serving 15 years in prison for the 1986 murder of Jennifer Levin.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Killing Fields
programme.desc: A report about a desolate strip of land between Houston and Galveston where over 30 young women and girls have disappeared or have been murdered.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Secrets of Waco
programme.desc: The 25th anniversary of the deadly standoff in Waco, Texas; a retired United Parcel Service driver reveals his experiences with cult leader David Koresh; a former ATF agent tells about his involvement.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Loved to Death
programme.desc: An investigation into the rise of violence surrounding relationship breakups among adolescents, as detailed in the story of Wayland, Massachusetts teen Lauren Astley, whose last encounter with her football player ex-boyfriend proved to be fatal

programme.title: 48 Hours: Ambushed
programme.desc: A man helps a new detective figure out who murdered his father.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Ghosts of Mississippi
programme.desc: A report on a string of unsolved homicides in Columbus, Mississippi. Five elderly residents, all living within a mile of each other, were killed within 18 months. Police say, a terrified community believes a serial killer is on the loose.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Last Chance
programme.desc: Attorneys work to get a death row inmate who confessed to a crime he didn't commit out of prison.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Deadly Secret
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the Kalamazoo, Michigan's cold case unit's investigation of the September 1994 disappearance of Khristine Smith, a seemingly happy wife and mother.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Ghosts of El Segundo
programme.desc: In July 1957 Richard Phillips and Milton Curtis, two California police officers on a routine stop, were gunned down by a man who had just committed robbery and rape nearby. Investigators searched for over 45 years for the man who murdered two of their own

programme.title: 48 Hours: Burden of Proof
programme.desc: Following the disappearance, in Jan. 1997, of 33-year-old Beverly Watson, her husband, Jim, became a prime suspect. Watson insisted that he was innocent. Police noticed that Jim was wearing makeup to conceal what appeared to be scratches on his face.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Justice For Sheila?
programme.desc: This report offers fresh insight into the brutal murder of Sheila Bellush, a Sarasota, Florida mother of quadruplets who was killed in front of her children.

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror
programme.desc: Borrowed from the world of fairy tales, giants have been found in stories from Jack and the Beanstalk to Jack the Giant Killer and beyond.

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: Carnival of Souls

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: 100 Years of Horror

programme.title: Slashers
programme.desc: Japanese TV's number one extreme reality show is having it's first American special! Six lucky contestants, chosen will have the chance to win millions of dollars. All they have to do is stay alive!

programme.title: LA Slasher
programme.desc: An anti-conspiracy video blogger thinks she is slipping into an alternate reality after being approached by an old man claiming to have worked for the legendary Majestic-12 (aka majic), created after the UFO incident at Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.

programme.title: Psycho Cop
programme.desc: Six college students on their weekend vacation are humiliated and killed one by one by a psycho cop.

programme.title: Slumber Party Massacre
programme.desc: A true horror cult classic! A high school student's slumber party turns terrifying as an escaped mental patient with a drill decides to crash the evening. Starring: Michelle Michaels, Robin Stille, Michael Villella
